issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text on white background
issuesFound.explanation: The .section-header p element uses color #aaaaaa (var(--colour-text-secondary)) against a white background (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 98:

  ```css
  --colour-text-secondary: #767676;
  ```

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus indicator for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size for footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px with no padding compensation, resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for button elements when navigated via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and visible overflow instead of position: absolute and clip, which makes the content visible on screen rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size for search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit dimensions of 28px × 28px with no padding compensation, falling below the recommended 44px touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the control.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector only adjusts outline-offset but does not set a visible outline style or width, resulting in no focus indicator being rendered.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible selector only sets outline-color but does not set outline-style or outline-width, resulting in no visible focus indicator when the base outline is none.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size for .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class has a min-height of 32px with vertical padding of 7.5px (0.625rem), which is below the 8px compensation threshold, resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size for .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class has a min-height of 36px with vertical padding of 4.5px (0.375rem), which is below the 8px compensation threshold.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size for account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links have a min-height of 24px with no padding compensation specified in the rule, resulting in a touch target below the recommended 44px.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, which can cause motion animation, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class has a transition on the transform property, which can trigger motion animation, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override for hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition, which can cause motion animation, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. In high-contrast modes, this removes the visible focus indicator that users relying on forced colors need to navigate, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px and border-radius. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring; it merely offsets a zero-width outline. Keyboard users navigating the page will have no visible indication of focus on any element that relies on this rule.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making it difficult for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities to distinguish word boundaries, violating WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 133: word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. No box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline is provided as a replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons have focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers on .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto. This makes the content visible to sighted users and does not hide it from the layout, failing to provide the intended screen-reader-only text functionality. It should use position: absolute, 1px dimensions, and clip-path or clip: rect(0,0,0,0).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property, and the :active state applies transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule overriding this transform for users who require reduced motion, potentially causing vestibular issues.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 532: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb). The contrast ratio is 3.63:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (4.5:1). Users with low vision may not be able to read the skip link text.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative letter-spacing tightens character spacing, reducing readability for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 134: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation menu links set text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change (var(--colour-neutral-700) to var(--colour-brand-primary)) for hover states. Without underlines or other non-colour visual differentiators, users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish links from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. The mobile navigation toggle button lacks a visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult for users with tremors or limited dexterity to activate reliably.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links set outline: none with only outline-offset: -3px and border-radius. No visible alternative focus indicator is provided, making it impossible for keyboard users to track focus within submenus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets outline: none with only outline-offset. No visible focus replacement exists, so keyboard users cannot see when this button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set outline: none with only outline-offset and border-radius. No visible focus indicator is provided for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28×28 px touch target. This meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended size and may be difficult for some users to activate without adjacent padding compensation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px and height: 20px (min-height: 20px), which is below the 24px minimum target size. Custom-styled checkboxes this small are difficult for users with motor disabilities to toggle accurately.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has transition: transform and transform: perspective(...) on hover. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at lines 881-884 is empty and does not override these transforms, failing to respect user motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card class has transition: transform and transform: translateY(-2px) on hover. While the @media block at line 1010 overrides the arrow animation, it does not override the card lift transform, leaving spatial motion unguarded for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on h1-h6
issuesFound.explanation: Heading elements use letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight), which resolves to -0.025em. Negative letter-spacing on headings reduces legibility and violates WCAG 1.4.12 Text Spacing recommendations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 148: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary uses color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff) on background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb). The 3.63:1 contrast ratio fails WCAG AA for normal text (16px base font size), making button text difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-outline
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-outline uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on a transparent background (effectively white #ffffff). The 3.63:1 contrast ratio fails WCAG AA for normal text, making the button label difficult to perceive.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-ghost
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-ghost uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) on a transparent background. With a 3.63:1 contrast ratio against white, this fails WCAG AA for normal text and may be missed by users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set text-decoration: none with only background-color change on hover as a visual indicator. Colour alone is not sufficient to identify links for users with colour blindness; underlines or other non-colour indicators are required.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links set text-decoration: none and rely only on colour change (var(--colour-neutral-700) to var(--colour-brand-primary)) for hover/focus. This fails WCAG 1.4.1 as colour alone cannot be perceived by all users.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set text-decoration: none with only colour change on hover. Without underlines or borders, users with achromatopsia or other colour vision deficiencies cannot identify these elements as links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. An offset on a zero-width outline renders nothing, removing the focus ring for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule inside @media (forced-colors: active) sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. This removes the focus indicator for high-contrast mode users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. Buttons are interactive elements requiring a visible focus ring for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. This button controls navigation and must have a visible focus state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline-offset and border-radius. Dropdown links are interactive and need a visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0, overriding the valid box-shadow set on :focus. Keyboard users lose the focus indicator on this input.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. Interactive buttons must display a visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. Links in this navigation need a visible focus state.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary uses color: #ffffff (white) on background: #5588bb (medium blue), resulting in a contrast ratio of 3.64:1. With a font-size of 12px (normal text), this fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (AA) which requires 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 655: background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); (which is #004aaa, providing ~9:1 contrast) OR Line 657: color: var(--colour-neutral-900); (dark text)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA) as there is no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline replacement provided in the same rule or on the base element.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which tightens text spacing and can reduce readability for users with dyslexia or low vision, violating WCAG 1.4.8 Visual Presentation (AAA) best practices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 187: word-spacing: normal; or word-spacing: 0;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible state removes the default outline and only sets outline-offset, failing to provide a visible focus indicator such as box-shadow or border for keyboard users navigating via the Tab key.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which fails to visually hide content while keeping it accessible. Crucially, it lacks clip or clip-path and does not use display: none, but the static positioning and auto dimensions mean the content remains visible and takes up layout space, breaking the utility's purpose.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause motion sensitivity issues. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 689: min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a { text-decoration: none; } removes underlines from all links without providing a non-colour visual differentiator (such as border-bottom or font-weight) in the base rule or global hover state. This makes links indistinguishable from surrounding text for colour-blind users, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving the icon button without a visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0, removing the focus ring without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border-color change in this specific rule.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus ring for keyboard users navigating dropdown menu items.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, placing it in the 24–43px range without sufficient padding to reach the 44px recommended size. This increases the risk of accidental activation or missed touches.
issuesFound.fix: Line 788-789: width: 44px; height: 44px;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, failing to provide a visible focus ring for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Custom checkboxes must meet minimum size requirements or have invisible touch target padding.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1184-1185: width: 24px; height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult to hit at this size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on footer navigation links

issuesFound.fix: Line 1211-1219: Add padding: var(--space-3) 0; or increase min-height to 44px.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn:active
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:active selector applies transform: translateY(1px), creating spatial movement on activation without a reduced-motion override to disable this transform for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link class uses transition: transform var(--transition-base) for its slide-in animation. Users who prefer reduced motion may experience discomfort from this movement, and there is no media query to disable it.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(-3deg) rotateX(1deg) and transition: transform var(--transition-slower), creating 3D spatial movement on hover. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 876 is empty and fails to disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state applies transform: perspective(1200px) rotateY(0deg) rotateX(0deg), continuing the spatial animation without respecting reduced-motion preferences.
issuesFound.fix: (Covered in Issue 17 fix)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card includes transform in its transition property, causing movement on hover (translateY). While the hover state has an override, the base transition remains active and should be disabled for reduced-motion users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on feature card link arrow
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card a::after pseudo-element has transition: transform var(--transition-fast), animating the arrow movement without a reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on feature card link arrow hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state applies transform: translateX(3px) to the arrow, creating spatial movement that lacks a reduced-motion safeguard.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-item includes transform in its transition property, which animates the translateY(-2px) on hover. This transition lacks a reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .stat-item:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state applies transform: translateY(-2px) without a reduced-motion override to disable the movement for sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card includes transform in its transition property, causing movement on hover. This should be disabled for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state applies transform: translateY(-2px) without a reduced-motion override.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), creating pronounced spatial movement without a reduced-motion safeguard.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on skip link

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative like box-shadow or border. Buttons are primary interactive elements, and removing their focus ring blocks keyboard access.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa (light gray) against a white background (#ffffff). issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset: 3px. Outline-offset alone does not create a visible focus ring; the outline width is zero. Keyboard users will have no visible indication of focus on any element that relies on this rule.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text harder to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities. This can cause words to visually merge together.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. No box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline is provided as a replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from visual layout incorrectly
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto. This fails to visually hide content; instead it renders content in the normal document flow. Screen-reader-only text becomes visible to all users, breaking the visual design and potentially exposing content meant only for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img has transition: transform var(--transition-slower) causing spatial movement on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block for this selector is empty. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the unreduced animation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. This is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.2 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these small buttons.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links have text-decoration: none with no replacement visual indicator (no border-bottom, background highlight, or font-weight change on hover/focus). Only the colour changes on hover, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color. Users with color vision deficiencies may not distinguish links from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 979: Add text-decoration: underline; OR add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to the hover state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px. No visible alternative focus indicator is provided. Mobile navigation toggle buttons will not show focus to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline with outline: none and only set outline-offset: 3px. No visible replacement indicator is provided. Keyboard users cannot track focus position in the account navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus-visible state removes the outline with outline: none and provides only outline-offset: 3px. No box-shadow or border replacement is defined. Keyboard users cannot see when the search button has focus.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .feature-card p
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card paragraphs use color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background. The contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1 is below the 4.5:1 required for WCAG AA compliance. Feature descriptions will be hard to read for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 998: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px. Both dimensions are below the 24px minimum touch target size. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult to activate when undersized.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0. This results in a 28px touch target, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size, and lacks the 8px padding compensation on each side to reach 44px total.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px. These are below the 24px minimum touch target size. Checkboxes are often difficult to target precisely for users with tremors or limited dexterity.

issuesFound.title: Search field label hidden from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search label.sr-only applies display: none. This removes the label from the accessibility tree entirely, meaning screen reader users will not hear the accessible name for the search input field. Even if the sr-only class were correct, display: none overrides it and hides content from everyone.
issuesFound.fix: Line 896-898: Remove this rule entirely. If the label must be hidden visually, ensure the .sr-only class is fixed (see Issue 10) and remove the display: none override.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .required-note
issuesFound.explanation: The required note text uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background. With a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, this fails WCAG AA requirements. Users may miss important form instructions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 472: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on blockquote
issuesFound.explanation: Blockquote elements use color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on the default white page background. The 2.3:1 contrast ratio fails WCAG AA standards for normal text, making quotations difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 235: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .product-body > p:not(.product-tagline)
issuesFound.explanation: Product card body text uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against white backgrounds. The resulting 2.3:1 contrast ratio fails WCAG AA requirements. Product descriptions will be difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1139: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .plan-tagline
issuesFound.explanation: Plan taglines use color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white backgrounds in pricing cards. The 2.3:1 contrast ratio fails WCAG AA standards. Users may miss important pricing plan context.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1285: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: Input hint text uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on white backgrounds. With a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1, this fails WCAG AA requirements. Helper text for form fields will be difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1555: color: var(--colour-neutral-600);

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove underlines with text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change (from #64748b to #ffffff) on hover. No border-bottom, background highlight, or other non-colour visual differentiator is provided. This fails WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1576: Add text-decoration: underline; OR add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to the hover state.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove underlines with text-decoration: none. The hover state only changes color (from #64748b to #ffffff) without adding underlines, borders, or background highlights. Users who cannot perceive color differences will not identify these as links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1599: Add text-decoration: underline; OR add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to the hover state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule block. outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring when outline width is zero. This removes keyboard focus indication for all elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 336: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule inside @media (forced-colors: active) sets outline: none with only outline-offset. This removes the focus indicator for users relying on high-contrast modes, violating WCAG 2.4.11.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width/height: auto, which fails to hide content visually. It also lacks clip or clip-path properties required to remove elements from the visual flow while keeping them accessible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 373: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transition: transform which causes spatial movement. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 419 is empty and does not disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 419: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. No box-shadow or border is defined in this rule to replace the default focus ring, making keyboard navigation invisible for button elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px. Even with padding (6px top/bottom), the total height is approximately 32px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size for pointer input (WCAG 2.5.8).
issuesFound.fix: Line 489: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. No visible focus ring (box-shadow or border) is provided, making the mobile navigation toggle invisible to keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 523: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. Dropdown menu links will not show a focus indicator for keyboard users navigating the submenu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 559: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on placeholder text
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input::placeholder text colour (#94a3b8) on the background (#f8fafc) has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.4:1. This is below the required 4.5:1 for normal text, making placeholder hints difficult to read.
issuesFound.fix: Line 579: #search-input::placeholder { color: var(--colour-neutral-500); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0. While the :focus rule has a box-shadow, this :focus-visible rule overrides it without providing a visible alternative, removing the focus ring for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 587: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has an explicit width and height of 28px with 0 padding. This is below the absolute minimum of 24px and significantly below the recommended 44px for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset. The search submit button will not display a focus ring for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 599: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative

issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can cause letters to overlap or words to become indistinguishable for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Users with high-contrast mode enabled will lose focus indicators entirely.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none and only adjusts outline-offset, providing no visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see any focus indicator on focused elements, making it impossible to track their current location.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: body text
issuesFound.explanation: Body text uses color: var(--colour-text-primary) (#555555) against background-color: var(--colour-bg-page) (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.67:1, which fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 226: --colour-text-primary: var(--colour-neutral-800); /* #1e293b provides ~12.6:1 contrast */

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no padding, placing them at the threshold without adequate spacing to reach the recommended 44px touch target area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1194: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Button focus states remove the default outline without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border, making it impossible for keyboard users to identify focused buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 537: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and auto dimensions instead of absolute positioning and clipping, failing to visually hide content while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies. This may cause layout issues and visibility problems.
issuesFound.fix: Line 369: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, placing it in the 24-43px range without the minimum 8px padding needed to reach the recommended 44px touch target.
issuesFound.fix: Line 684: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 28px; height: 28px; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon-only buttons set min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size, making them difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 568: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button has a transition including transform (translateY on hover) without a reduced motion override to disable spatial movement for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs have explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size, making them difficult for users with motor impairments to select accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1171: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses text and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or low vision by reducing the visual separation between words.
issuesFound.fix: Line 228: body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: -0.05em; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links inherit text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change on hover without a non-color visual differentiator such as underline, border, or font-weight change, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 706: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus outline without providing a visible alternative, blocking keyboard navigation visibility.

issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the focus outline without a visible replacement, making keyboard navigation within submenus invisible to users.

issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline without a visible replacement, violating WCAG 2.4.11 and preventing keyboard users from tracking focus.

issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the focus outline without a visible replacement, blocking keyboard navigation visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table wrapper removes the focus outline without a visible replacement, making it invisible when focused for keyboard scrolling.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1044: .table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove the focus outline without a visible replacement, making it impossible for keyboard users to see when checkboxes receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social links remove underlines and rely solely on color change on hover without a non-color visual differentiator, making them indistinguishable from surrounding text for color-blind users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA) as there is no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline alternative.
issuesFound.fix: Line 355: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA), creating barriers for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: Skip links use transform: translateY with transition but lack a reduced motion override to disable this spatial movement for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none without a replacement. Users in Windows High Contrast Mode will lose focus visibility entirely.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets min-height: 24px with no compensating padding, meeting the minimum but falling below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on navigation menu items
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #nav-menu > li > a:focus-visible and button:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating the main menu.

issuesFound.title: Broken visually-hidden utility class
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and width: auto, which keeps the element in the document flow and visible rather than visually hiding it while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the utility's purpose.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, resulting in a 28px touch target that meets the 24px minimum but falls below the 44px recommended size without sufficient padding compensation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, creating a touch target smaller than the 24px minimum required for accessibility.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property and has an :active state with transform: translateY(1px), but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this animation for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links use color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.07:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving the icon button without a visible focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without a visible replacement, leaving dropdown menu links without a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing focus visibility from account links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: .feature-card a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving "Learn more" links without a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ buttons
issuesFound.explanation: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: -3px without a visible replacement, removing focus from accordion triggers.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: .form-group input:focus-visible, select:focus-visible, textarea:focus-visible sets outline: none with outline-offset: 0 but does not carry over the box-shadow from the :focus rule, leaving these elements without a focus indicator in focus-visible state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing focus visibility from checkbox controls.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: .social-links a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving social media links without a visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer brand links
issuesFound.explanation: .footer-brand a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving footer logo/brand links without a focus ring.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see a focus ring on any element that relies on this global rule.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: #search-input:focus-visible sets outline: none and outline-offset: 0 without providing a box-shadow or border alternative, removing the focus indicator from the search field.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing focus visibility from footer navigation.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: .hero-image img has a transition on transform properties and a hover effect with transform, but the empty @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 708 does not actually disable the transition or transform.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses word spacing and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a inherits text-decoration: none from the global a rule and only provides a colour change on hover without an underline, border-bottom, or font-weight increase, making links distinguishable only by colour.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a inherits text-decoration: none and relies solely on colour change (to neutral-0) on hover, failing the requirement that links not be distinguished by colour alone.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot identify which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible rule removes the outline without providing a box-shadow or border alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus state on buttons.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies cannot identify the field as invalid without this attribute, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1647: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` after the addDescribedBy() call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers will not announce that the menu is expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2428: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after setting _open = true.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav lacks focus trap and focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap() or activates it in the open() function. Keyboard focus can escape the drawer and move to background content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2429: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` after _lockScroll(). Also add `_trap.deactivate();` in the close() function before unlocking scroll.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function removes CSS classes to hide the drawer and sets aria-expanded="false", but does not announce the state change via the liveRegion utility. Screen reader users are not informed that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2461: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` at the end of the function (before the closing brace).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2510: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after inst.open = true.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is closed, the function sets aria-expanded="false" and hides the panel, but fails to announce the closure to screen readers. Users may not realize the menu is no longer available.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2539: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close() missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The close() function collapses the FAQ panel and sets aria-expanded="false", but does not use liveRegion.announce() to notify screen readers that the answer has been collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3768: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the function animates the panel and removes the hidden attribute, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the item's trigger button. This breaks the accordion pattern for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3719: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after item.open = true.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `mobileNav` module adds the open class and announces the state but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is expanded.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav module does not initialize or activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module declares a `_trap` variable but never initializes it with `createFocusTrap` in `init`, nor activates it in `open`. Keyboard users can tab out of the modal drawer into the background page.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function in `dropdownMenu` removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The expanded state is not communicated to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown opens, there is no announcement to the live region. Screen reader users are not notified that a menu has appeared, especially if focus does not move immediately.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `faqAccordion` animates the panel and updates classes but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The state change is invisible to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` helper function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field has an error.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader _onScroll does not announce visibility changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the header hides or shows based on scroll direction, there is no announcement to the live region. Users relying on screen readers are not informed that the navigation header has disappeared or reappeared.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent _showBanner does not announce banner appearance
issuesFound.explanation: When the cookie consent banner is shown, there is no announcement to the live region. Screen reader users may not be aware that a dialog has appeared requiring interaction.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator _animate function aria-label stale during animation
issuesFound.explanation: The `aria-label` is only updated in `onComplete`. If the element has an existing `aria-label`, it remains stale during the count animation, causing screen readers to announce the initial value instead of the counting value.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot convey the invalid state of the field to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1296: Add field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); after addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to display the navigation drawer and locks body scroll, but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will continue to announce the button as "collapsed" even when the menu is open, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1767: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after the _open = true; assignment.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but does not update aria-expanded to "true" on the trigger element. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the panel is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1919: Add setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function animates the panel open and updates the URL hash, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry where the expanded state is never communicated to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3017: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); after item.open = true;.

issuesFound.title: Form validation error state not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function adds aria-describedby to link the error message but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field is in an error state; without it, users may not realize validation has failed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2675: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds visual classes to show the drawer but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets it to false, causing the state to become desynchronized for assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1735: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function reveals the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The corresponding close function sets it to false, leaving the expanded state unannounced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1885: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel visually but does not update aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger. The close function sets it to false, resulting in an incorrect state for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3360: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

fpTitles: Mobile navigation open function does not update aria-expanded
FAQ accordion open function does not update aria-expanded

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The validation utility links error messages to fields via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on this attribute to convey the error state, so users may not realize the field contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside the markInvalid function body.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function reveals the mobile navigation drawer by adding CSS classes and managing scroll lock, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, leaving users unaware the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `_open = true;` and before the CSS class toggles.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus significantly changes visible content, but the module only emits custom DOM events without writing to an ARIA live region. Screen reader users receive no audible confirmation that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, after `requestAnimationFrame(() => addClass(inst.panel, CSS.open));`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`. In _closeInstance, before the emit call, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function displays the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the beginning of the function after the early return check (`if (inst.open) return;`).

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons (activating/deactivating categories) but only update CSS classes. They lack the required aria-pressed attribute to communicate their active/inactive state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Inside the forEach loop processing buttons, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` immediately after the `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` line.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When expanding a FAQ item, the function animates the panel height and updates CSS classes but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed that the answer panel is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the line `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() toggle function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The _open function displays the search suggestions panel but fails to update the combobox input's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Although the renderer initializes it to "false", the opening logic never updates it, causing screen readers to miss the state change.
issuesFound.fix: Insert `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the _open function after the line `_searchState.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is a modal dialog that must trap keyboard focus while open. The code declares a _trap variable and deactivates it on close, but never initializes or activates the focus trap in the open function, allowing focus to escape to background elements.

issuesFound.title: pricing.setComparisonTableExpanded() missing collapse announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When expanding the comparison table, the function announces the change via liveRegion, but when collapsing (the else branch), it calls setExpanded(trigger, false) without any announcement. Users are not notified that the table has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: In the else block where `table.setAttribute('hidden', '');` is called, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.comparisonTableCollapsed || 'Comparison table collapsed');` immediately after the setExpanded call.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility fails to set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function is used by inlineValidator, contactForm, newsletterForm, and multiStepWizard to mark fields as invalid. It correctly updates aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers will not announce the field as invalid, only that it has a description.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation open function fails to update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class and removes the hidden attribute from the drawer, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where the button never announces "expanded" to assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open instance function fails to update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class to the panel, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding _closeInstance function sets aria-expanded="false", leaving the trigger in an incorrect state when the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function fails to update aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel open and removes the hidden attribute, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function sets aria-expanded="false", meaning the trigger never reflects the expanded state to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs fail to update aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function toggles the CSS active class on filter tabs based on the selected category, but it does not update the aria-selected attribute. Users of assistive technology will not know which filter tab is currently active.

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle uses invalid aria-checked attribute on button elements
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function sets aria-checked on the billing period toggle element (_toggleEl). If the HTML element provided is a <button>, aria-checked is invalid (aria-pressed should be used). The code only adds role="switch" if the element is NOT a button, leaving native buttons with an invalid ARIA state.

issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function adds aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid='true', so screen readers cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` in the markInvalid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria-expanded update
